commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A repair worker can address the narrow test-mock and docs wording blockers; gateway/security owner acceptance remains a human merge decision afterward.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally expands the Gateway security boundary by allowing opt-in host filesystem reads over HTTP/RPC direct invoke; no hidden supply-chain change was found.; Review finding: [P1] Export the raw coding-tool mock — src/gateway/to... (sha=677f991b0c300cfc19969662d98abf7aefb41f31)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a dual-key `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead` plus `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` gate to expose the coding `read` tool through Gateway HTTP/RPC direct invoke, with config schema/help/labels, security audit coverage, tests, and docs.

PR surface: Source +191, Tests +103, Docs +52. Total +346 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking PR defect: the merge-result source shows a new named import from `../agents/agent-tools.js` while the existing Vitest mock for that module omits the export. I did not run tests because this review is read-only and the source path is sufficient to identify the import/mock mismatch.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can address the narrow test-mock and docs wording blockers; gateway/security owner acceptance remains a human merge decision afterward.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire read coding tool into HTTP /tools/invoke (narrow)
target.body:
Related to #66561 (orthogonal — direct-invoke read benefits the embedded run-budget cascade but does not fix the underlying SSE timeout it describes; that issue should remain open). narrow first landing of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Wire the `read` coding tool into the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver) so it is reachable for deterministic automation — CI/preflight checks, lint pipelines, browser capture flows — without an LLM round-trip.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow read-only landing.** The prior 10-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of current upstream `7c1a83ff2e`, restructured to satisfy ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review: **dual-key gating** so the existing `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` config knob does not silently grant host-FS read on upgrade.

## Dual-key gating (addresses ClawSweeper [P1])

To enable `read` on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` | **NEW.** Distinct surface-specific opt-in. Without this, `read` is not materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes it. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` | Lifts `read` from `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (now extended with `"read"`). Existing knob; keeps operator-acceptance step visible in the standard policy surface. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves `read` unreachable.** Old configs that already contain `allow: ["read"]` for unrelated reasons (e.g. an MCP/agent surface where `read` was always available) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer — no silent upgrade-time expansion.

| `tools.allow` includes `"read"` | `directInvoke.hostFsRead` | `read` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ❌ |
| yes | no   | ❌ (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | ❌ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Read-only.** Mutating coding primitives (`write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` / `exec` / `process`) are NOT exposed by this PR.
- **Both surfaces.** Gating applies to HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND the SDK-facing JSON-RPC `tools.invoke` (they share `resolveGatewayScopedTools`).
- **MCP loopback unaffected.** `surface === "loopback"` is not gated.
- **Hook-bypass safe.** Uses new `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export (module-private Symbol marker, not reachable from typed SDK params) so HTTP-surface tools aren't double-wrapped — `handleToolsInvokeHttp` already runs `runBeforeToolCallHook` itself before dispatch.

## Files changed

- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — `SKIP_BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK` symbol + `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export
- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — `GatewayToolsConfig.directInvoke: GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` field (new type)
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — `"read"` added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — dual-key gated materialization of `read` into candidate set
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` finding (only fires when BOTH gates set; silent for either alone since `read` is unreachable there)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — 5 new test cases covering all 4 gate combinations + vanilla
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` — operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs with config example + truth table

## Real-behavior proof on head `677f991b0c`

**Build:** `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`. Output bundle `dist/tool-resolution-CEUHGjuC.js` confirmed to contain `directInvoke` + `hostFsRead` strings (also in `dist/audit-DbRnDesk.js`).

**Driver:** Node ESM script imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from the freshly-compiled bundle (resolved by `fn.name`), calls it with 4 config shapes, asserts whether `read` is in the returned tools list.

```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-CEUHGjuC.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[CASE 1: neither gate set (vanilla)]                                     read=false  tools=23  ✅
[CASE 2: only allow:['read'] set (legacy shape)]                         read=false  tools=23  ✅
[CASE 3: only directInvoke.hostFsRead=true set]                          read=false  tools=23  ✅
[CASE 4: BOTH allow:['read'] AND directInvoke.hostFsRead=true]           read=true   tools=24  ✅

=== ALL PASS ===
```

CASE 2 is the critical bot-P1 case: a legacy config with `allow: ["read"]` (for whatever historical reason) stays inert at the direct-invoke layer post-merge — no silent host-FS read grant. CASE 4 is the only combination that grants access.

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block):

- ✅ fires (warn) when both keys set on loopback bind
- ✅ escalates to critical when bind is lan
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `allow: ["read"]` (legacy shape)
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `hostFsRead: true`
- ✅ does NOT fire on vanilla config

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 10)

The original PR's 10 commits implemented an OLD design (single-key `allow: ["read"]` as the opt-in). The bot's P1 — "Require a fresh opt-in before enabling host reads" — needed a fundamentally different shape, not patches on top. Plus the prior commits referenced `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` which has been renamed to `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` upstream. Cleaner to reset to upstream/main and write the redesign as one commit than rebase-then-patch.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/85664-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-7c1a83ff2e` on the public fork.

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Expose the `read` coding tool over Gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver) for deterministic automation, with a NEW distinct `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` opt-in that gates host-FS read materialization. Addresses ClawSweeper [P1] on the prior PR head (single-key `allow: ["read"]` would have silently granted host-FS read on upgrade for legacy configs).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `git checkout 677f991b0c && docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work --platform linux/arm64 --memory=12g node:22-bookworm-slim bash -c 'set -e; corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@11.2.2 --activate && node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node /work/.proof-driver-85664.mjs'` — exits 0 with `=== ALL PASS ===`.

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from current PR head `677f991b0c` inside a clean throwaway `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container — workspace bind-mounted, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs` produced `dist/tool-resolution-CEUHGjuC.js`. Driver imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from that bundle and probes the 4 config combinations.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted):**
```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-CEUHGjuC.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[CASE 1: neither gate set (vanilla)]
  config: {}
  read reachable: false
  total tools: 23

[CASE 2: only allow:['read'] set (legacy shape)]
  config: {"allowRead":true}
  read reachable: false
  total tools: 23

[CASE 3: only directInvoke.hostFsRead=true set]
  config: {"hostFsRead":true}
  read reachable: false
  total tools: 23

[CASE 4: BOTH allow:['read'] AND directInvoke.hostFsRead=true]
  config: {"allowRead":true,"hostFsRead":true}
  read reachable: true
  total tools: 24

=== SUMMARY ===
  PASS  CASE 1: neither gate set (vanilla)  (read=false)
  PASS  CASE 2: only allow:['read'] set (legacy shape)  (read=false)
  PASS  CASE 3: only directInvoke.hostFsRead=true set  (read=false)
  PASS  CASE 4: BOTH allow:['read'] AND directInvoke.hostFsRead=true  (read=true)

=== ALL PASS ===
```

**Observed result after fix (head `677f991b0c`):** All 4 cases PASS. CASE 2 — the bot-P1 critical case — confirms a legacy config with `allow: ["read"]` alone does NOT grant host-FS read on the direct-invoke surface; the new `directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` opt-in is required additionally.

**What was not tested:** Live HTTP request through `handleToolsInvokeHttp` to a running gateway. The `resolveGatewayScopedTools` driver tests the candidate-set construction (where the dual-key gating lives); the downstream policy filter + HTTP dispatch are upstream-tested code paths that operate on whatever candidate set this function returns.




## Live HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` proof

Addresses ClawSweeper's [P1]: "Post redacted current-head live HTTP /tools/invoke or SDK RPC tools.invoke read proof covering default deny, each single-key deny, and both-key success."

Built `dist/` fresh from head `677f991b0c` in `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, started the gateway via `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 18900 --auth token --token <secret>`, mutated `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` per case, and `POST /tools/invoke` with `node fetch` (curl not installed in slim image) targeting a sentinel file. Stop + restart between cases.

**Results — 4 / 4 PASS:**

```
====================================================
LIVE HTTP /tools/invoke proof for PR #85664 (read)
head: 677f991b0c
====================================================

CASE 1: neither gate set (default-deny)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=false
  [gateway] ready (pid=49, attempt=4)
  --- POST /tools/invoke {tool:read,args:{path:/tmp/proof-target.txt}} ---
    HTTP 404
    body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

CASE 2: only allow=["read"] (legacy)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["read"] hostFsRead=false
  [gateway] ready (pid=97, attempt=4)
  --- POST /tools/invoke {tool:read,args:{path:/tmp/proof-target.txt}} ---
    HTTP 404
    body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

CASE 3: only hostFsRead=true (single key)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=true
  [gateway] ready (pid=145, attempt=4)
  --- POST /tools/invoke {tool:read,args:{path:/tmp/proof-target.txt}} ---
    HTTP 404
    body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

CASE 4: BOTH keys (success expected)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["read"] hostFsRead=true
  [gateway] ready (pid=193, attempt=4)
  --- POST /tools/invoke {tool:read,args:{path:/tmp/proof-target.txt}} ---
    HTTP 200
    body: {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF FILE CONTENTS for live HTTP test on PR #85664\n"}]}}
```

**Critical observation:** CASE 2 (legacy `allow:["read"]` only) returns HTTP **404 "Tool not available: read"** — same as the no-key default-deny CASE 1. The dual-key gating prevents the documented bot [P1] concern: existing configs that already include `"read"` in `allow` (for an unrelated MCP/agent surface) stay inert here, no silent host-FS read grant on upgrade. CASE 4 confirms the intended path is reachable when BOTH keys are explicitly set, returning the actual file contents over HTTP.

## Docs discoverability

Addresses ClawSweeper's [P2]: "Document the host-FS read opt-in everywhere" (`src/config/zod-schema.ts:1037-1040`).

- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — FIELD_HELP entries for `gateway.tools.directInvoke` and `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead`.
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — paragraph describing the dual-key gating + audit warning hookup.
- `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — catalog row for `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` with severity, criteria, and config-key references.

So operators using config help/lookup or security-audit docs can now discover this security-sensitive opt-in and warning.
target.author: simonusa

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
